subtasks.description: Query IAM system to retrieve all roles with elevated permissions, including role ID, role name, and complete permission manifest (granted permissions list)
Query IAM audit logs (MongoDB) to extract permissions actually exercised by each role in the last 180 days, aggregating into set of used permissions per role

Reconcile granted permissions (from sql_gen_elevated_roles) against exercised permissions (from mongo_query_exercised_perms). For each elevated role, compute set difference (granted - exercised) to identify never-used permissions. Classify each unused permission by risk tier (critical, high, medium, low). Return flagged permissions as revocation candidates with justification.

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks are necessary: (1) sql_gen retrieves the full grant manifest from IAM (mid tier: multi-table join of roles + permissions tables), (2) mongo_query aggregates audit logs across 180 days to find exercised permissions per role (mid tier: complex $group/$lookup pipeline over large event collection), (3) cross_recon performs set reconciliation, identifies unused permissions, and classifies them by security risk for revocation recommendation (frontier tier: multi-signal matching across two large datasets with classification logic and risk assessment). The cost justifies frontier for cross_recon because the reconciliation must handle potentially thousands of permissions per role and assign risk-based classification to drive actionable policy changes.

output.sql: SELECT al.permission, al.resource, COUNT(DISTINCT al.user_id) as user_count, COUNT(DISTINCT al.access_time::DATE) as access_days, MAX(al.access_time) as last_access FROM access_log_facts al WHERE al.access_time >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '180 days' AND al.result = 'success' GROUP BY al.permission, al.resource ORDER BY access_days DESC, user_count DESC
